finding: §8.3 item 3 says the deduplicated previous-findings summary is 'injected into every prompt', and §11.2 makes the auto-appended regression prompt the 4th entry of prompts — but §11.3 defines the regression prompt's body order exhaustively with no findings-summary block. Two testers assert contradictory prompt contents on the default round-2 path.

resolved.evidence: Round-10 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md: §8.3 findings-summary scoped to role prompts (§11.3 exhaustive); §8.4 wontfix validation boundary pinned (empty evidence → exit 1; verifier-naming = SKILL convention), duplicate disposition defined (dirties), clean flag frozen at record time; §8.2/§8.3/§11.1 regression state participation conditioned on mode (a); same batch adds review_max_rounds/audit_max_rounds round budgets (§8.8, escalation-only semantics) per user decision

suggestion: Scope §8.3 item 3 to role prompts; the regression prompt carries only its §11.3 blocks.

finding: 'evidence MUST name the verifier's reasoning' is a normative requirement no mechanical rule backs — --record validation of pre-resolved wontfix rows is unspecified, so a self-certified wontfix with empty evidence either records clean or aborts depending on reading; convergence becomes reachable through unverified self-certification.

finding: Special handling covers wontfix and fixed but not duplicate — enum-incompleteness pattern.

finding: Item 3 protects only the recorded count; with clean now resolution-derived, whether the clean flag is frozen must be inferred. §16 item 2 vs 12 ordering leaves no recovery path for post-record wontfix.
